Here is the "Routing" section from the SvelteKit docs (explains filesystem-based routing and routing behavior):

## Routing

At the heart of SvelteKit is a _filesystem-based router_. The routes of your app — i.e. the URL paths that users can access — are defined by the directories in your codebase:

- `src/routes` is the root route
- `src/routes/about` creates an `/about` route
- `src/routes/blog/[slug]` creates a route with a _parameter_, `slug`, that can be used to load data dynamically when a user requests a page like `/blog/hello-world`

> [!NOTE] You can change `src/routes` to a different directory by editing the [project config](configuration).

Each route directory contains one or more _route files_, which can be identified by their `+` prefix.

We'll introduce these files in a moment in more detail, but here are a few simple rules to help you remember how SvelteKit's routing works:

* All files can run on the server
* All files run on the client except `+server` files
* `+layout` and `+error` files apply to subdirectories as well as the directory they live in

(After this the docs continue into the +page, +error and +layout sections describing route files and behavior.)

  - That returns an object mapping versions to publish timestamps; look up the timestamp for the version returned by the previous command.

3) Single-call using the npm registry (gives both dist-tags and times)

Notes:
- The “latest” dist-tag is the stable release in most cases; pre-releases are usually tagged differently (like next, beta, etc.). If you want to be extra safe, check the version string for pre-release labels (e.g., 5.0.0-beta.1).
- If you run npm view svelte time --json, the output includes an entry like "2024-03-10T12:34:56.789Z" for each version.

Commands (API):

1) Get default branch name:
curl -s https://api.github.com/repos/facebook/react | jq -r '.default_branch'

2) Get the most recent commit on that branch (replace BRANCH if you ran step 1):
curl -s "https://api.github.com/repos/facebook/react/commits?sha=BRANCH&per_page=1" | jq -r '.[0] | "\(.commit.message)\nAuthor: \(.commit.author.name) <\(.commit.author.email)>\nSHA: \(.sha)\nURL: \(.html_url)"'

Or a one-liner that uses the repo’s default branch implicitly:
curl -s "https://api.github.com/repos/facebook/react/commits?per_page=1" | jq -r '.[0] | "\(.commit.message)\nAuthor: \(.commit.author.name) <\(.commit.author.email)>\nSHA: \(.sha)\nURL: \(.html_url)"'

If you prefer the web UI, visit:

1) Provide an API key and confirm I should use it here (I’ll call the API and return a list of project IDs, titles and creation dates), or
2) Run a short curl/jq command locally (no token needed for public projects on the demo) and paste the output here — I’ll parse and present a neat list.

If you prefer option 2, run one of these commands and paste the output:

- If you want to try unauthenticated (public projects only):
  curl -s 'https://demo.openproject.org/api/v3/projects?pageSize=100' | jq '.'

- To get a compact tab-separated list of id, title and creation date (if present):
  curl -s 'https://demo.openproject.org/api/v3/projects?pageSize=100' \
    | jq -r '.elements[] | [(.id // ""), (.name // ""), (.createdAt // .createdOn // "" )] | @tsv'

- If you choose to use an API token (recommended if some projects are restricted), first create a personal access token in your demo.openproject.org account, then run:
  curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer <API_TOKEN>" 'https://demo.openproject.org/api/v3/projects?pageSize=100' \
    | jq -r '.elements[] | [(.id // ""), (.name // ""), (.createdAt // .createdOn // "" )] | @tsv'

Tell me which option you want (paste the token here if you want me to call the API), or paste the curl output and I’ll extract and format the projects for you.
